seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programming Agentic RAG systems by composing optimizable modules for query planning, multi-source retrieval, and synthesis with attribution. These autonomous agents excel at complex reasoning but risk hallucinations, unfaithful citations, and flawed query plans that erode trust. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures, ensuring production-grade reliability without retraining.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
Agentic RAG with DSPy typically starts with a Router module that plans optimal queries based on user intent, followed by parallel Retriever modules querying vector stores, web search, and knowledge graphs. A Synthesizer module then grades retrieved contexts for relevance, merges cross-source information, and generates cited responses. An optional Critic module self-evaluates for hallucinations and citation accuracy, enabling iterative refinement.

DSPy's optimization loop tunes these modules against metrics like source faithfulness and answer correctness. The agent's autonomy comes from ChainOfThought and ReAct patterns, but this introduces risks of reasoning drift and composition failures. Vex wraps critical forward() calls to verify outputs match architectural intent.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    ↓
[Router/Planner] ──→ Query Plan
    ↓
[Parallel Retrievers]
    ├── Vector DB ──→ ctx1
    ├── Web Search ─→ ctx2  
    └── KG ────────→ ctx3
    ↓
[Synthesizer + Citer] ──→ Cited Response
    ↓
[Critic] ───────────────→ Verified Output

risks.name: Source Faithfulness Drift
risks.description: Optimized synthesizer modules paraphrase retrieved content instead of faithfully extracting, especially after DSPy optimization
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between citations and claims

risks.name: Query Planning Hallucinations
risks.description: Router generates plausible but irrelevant subqueries that retrieve noise
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies query relevance against original intent

risks.name: Cross-Source Inconsistency
risks.description: Synthesizer fabricates consensus when sources conflict
risks.mitigation: Vex consistency scoring across retrieved contexts

risks.name: Citation Fabrication
risks.description: Agent cites non-existent or misrepresented sources
risks.mitigation: Vex exact-match verification of cited chunks

implementation.title: DSPy Router Module for Query Planning
implementation.description: Basic ReAct-style planner that decomposes complex questions into subqueries

implementation.title: Multi-Source Retriever
implementation.description: Parallel retrieval from vector DB and web search with relevance grading

implementation.title: Faithful Synthesizer with Attribution
implementation.description: Generates answers strictly from retrieved contexts with inline citations

implementation.title: Self-Critique Module
implementation.description: Evaluates generated answer for hallucinations and citation accuracy

vexIntegration.title: Verify Source Faithfulness
vexIntegration.description: Wrap synthesizer to ensure generated claims match retrieved contexts

vexIntegration.title: Validate Query Planning
vexIntegration.description: Ensure planned subqueries align with original user intent

vexIntegration.title: Cross-Source Consistency Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify synthesized answer doesn't contradict any source

cta.heading: Secure Your DSPy Agentic RAG
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your DSPy modules in 3 lines of code and eliminate hallucination risk today.
